description: This group of X-ray diffraction characterization results focuses on phase evolution of Fe₄₀Mn₁₉Ni₁₅Al₁₅Si₁₀C₁ high entropy steel powders during mechanical alloying. Subfigure (a) shows full-range (2θ = 20°–100°) XRD patterns of samples milled for 0 h to 35 h, tracking the decay of elemental Fe, Mn, Ni, Al, Si, C diffraction peaks as milling progresses. Subfigure (b) gives an enlarged view of the 2θ = 49°–54° region to resolve subtle peak changes during phase formation. Subfigure (c) displays the detailed XRD pattern of the 35 h milled sample, identifying coexisting phases: dominant BCC phase, γ-brass type phase, B2-type ordered intermetallic phase, and trace residual Si. Subfigure (d) provides Rietveld refinement results of the 35 h sample XRD data to validate phase composition and structural parameters. Key trends include sequential dissolution of alloying elements (Al fully dissolves after 15 h, Ni/Mn after 20 h), solid solution formation after 25 h, and formation of the expected nanocrystalline multiphase structure after 35 h of milling, supporting the effectiveness of the adopted mechanical alloying process.
